advanced.factoryResetConfirmDetailed:
Are you sure you want to perform a TOTAL RESET?

This will erase ABSOLUTELY EVERYTHING:
• All asset library
• Job and log history
• All custom profiles
• All your settings

The application will restart. This action is irreversible.

dropZone.clickToSelect: Or click to select files (MP4, MKV, MOV, MXF...)

intro.description: Nexora Desktop is a native multiplatform application for professional media processing. It brings the power of the Nexora server pipeline directly to your workstation, with zero cloud dependencies.

features.offline: Fully offline processing — no internet required
features.vmaf: Active VMAF quality scoring with per-profile thresholds
features.transcodingDesc: FFmpeg with GPU acceleration (NVENC, AMF, QSV) or CPU (libx264)
features.vmafDesc: Perceptual quality score 0-100 for each job
features.profilesDesc: Broadcast HD/SD, Web HD/4K, Social, Proxy
features.gpuAutoDesc: Automatic detection of NVIDIA, AMD and Intel

intro.architecture: Built with Tauri 2.x (Rust) for the native shell, React 19 for the UI, and a Node.js sidecar for media orchestration. SQLite stores all metadata locally.
intro.quickStartDesc: Go to Library → drag a video file → click Reprocess → select a profile → monitor in Queue → review results in Asset Detail.

steps.1: Add media via drag & drop or file dialog
steps.2: Select a transcode profile (broadcast-hd, web-4k, etc.)
steps.3: Submit to the queue — jobs process automatically
steps.4: Review QC reports and approve/reject quarantined items
steps.5: Access processed files in the output folder

tipsList.1: Use GPU acceleration for 5-10x faster transcodes
tipsList.2: Enable notifications to get alerts when jobs finish
tipsList.3: Check the Logs page for detailed FFmpeg output
tipsList.4: Factory Reset wipes everything — use with caution

usage.step1Desc: Click the rocket icon in the navigation bar to access the processing page.
usage.step2Desc: Drag an MP4, MXF, MOV or any FFmpeg-supported format to the drop zone. You can also click to open the file picker.
usage.step3Desc: Choose the profile appropriate for the final destination (broadcast, web, social). Each profile has pre-configured parameters for compliance and optimal quality.
usage.step4Desc: Click Process. The job is added to the queue and processed in the background. The app remains responsive during processing.
usage.step5Desc: The Dashboard shows active jobs, average VMAF score and disk space. The progress bar updates in real time.
usage.step6Desc: After completion, the asset appears in History with codec, duration, size and VMAF score. Use the details button to see all metadata.
usage.tip1: Enable GPU in Settings for 5-10x faster transcoding
usage.tip2: The Proxy profile is ideal for creating editing versions — never for final delivery
usage.tip3: Save logs in Settings → Logs for troubleshooting
usage.tip4: The output directory can be set globally or per profile in Settings

profiles.title: Available encoding profiles

broadcastHd.name: Broadcast HD
broadcastHd.desc: Full broadcast compliance — H.264 High 4.1, 50 Mbps, -23 LUFS

webHd.name: Web HD
webHd.desc: Optimized for web and streaming — H.264, 8 Mbps

proxy.name: Proxy
proxy.desc: Fast offline editing — 720p, 2 Mbps, low latency

social.name: Social
social.desc: Social media — vertical and square formats

profiles.note: System profiles cannot be edited. Duplicate them to create custom variants.

metrics.title: Quality metrics
metrics.vmaf: VMAF (Video Multi-Method Assessment Fusion) scores perceptual quality from 0-100. Thresholds: 85 (web), 90 (broadcast SD), 93 (broadcast HD/4K).
metrics.lufs: LUFS (Loudness Units Full Scale) measures audio loudness. Target: -23 LUFS for broadcast, -14 LUFS for web/social.
metrics.truePeak: True Peak (dBTP) prevents digital clipping. Limit: -1 dBTP for all profiles.
metrics.realtime: Real-time metrics update every 2 seconds during active jobs.
metrics.vmafDescription: Perceptual video quality metric developed by Netflix. Simulates human perception by comparing the processed video with the original.
metrics.lufsDescription: Integrated loudness measure according to EBU R128 standard. The standard target for European broadcast is −23 LUFS ±1 LU. For streaming (Spotify, YouTube) the target is typically −14 LUFS.
metrics.ramDesc: RAM — memory used / total in GB
metrics.networkDesc: Network ↓↑ — aggregated download and upload throughput of all interfaces

system.stackFrontend: Frontend: React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS 4 + Zustand

support.factoryResetWarning: This will delete ALL data, settings, and history. The app will restart. This action cannot be undone.

problems.1: No GPU detected — Install latest graphics drivers
problems.2: Jobs failing at transcode — Verify FFmpeg binary is bundled

support.factoryResetDescription: If the app shows inconsistent behavior, data that won't clear, or database errors, you can perform a Total Reset in Settings → Advanced.
support.problem1Desc: Check if FFmpeg is correctly detected in the System tab.
support.problem2Desc: Make sure Nexora has write permissions to the configured folder.

about.footer: Nexora Media Processing — Professional media pipeline for broadcast and web.
about.reportIssues: To report problems or suggestions, check the system logs and include the relevant output in your report.

dashboard.title: Dashboard — System Overview
dashboard.desc: The Dashboard shows your system health at a glance: total assets, jobs completed today, and average VMAF score.
dashboard.stat1: Total Assets — all media files in your library
dashboard.stat2: Jobs Today — completed jobs in the last 24 hours
dashboard.stat3: Average VMAF — mean quality score of recent outputs
dashboard.recentJobs: Recent Jobs — click any job to open its Asset Detail
dashboard.tip2: Click any job in the Recent Jobs list to jump to Asset Detail.
dashboard.tip3: The VMAF Distribution bar shows quality tiers at a glance.

library.title: Library — Asset Management
library.desc: The Library is your media repository. Ingest files via drag-and-drop or file dialog, then manage, preview, and reprocess them.
library.dragDrop: Drag & drop files from anywhere on your OS
library.gridList: Toggle between Grid and List views
library.searchFilter: Search by filename and filter by status
library.reprocess: Click any asset to reprocess with a different profile
library.tip1: Supported formats: MP4, MKV, MOV, MXF, AVI, WebM, TS, M2TS.
library.tip2: Hover over a grid card to reveal Open and Play buttons.
library.tip3: The view mode (Grid/List) is remembered between sessions.

queue.title: Queue — Job Monitoring
queue.desc: The Queue shows all jobs in real time. Watch the 8-stage pipeline visualizer, approve quarantined jobs, and retry failures.
queue.quarantine: Quarantined jobs appear in Pending Approvals
queue.approveReject: Approve to continue, or Reject to cancel
queue.retry: Failed jobs can be retried with one click
queue.tip2: Green check = done, Blue pulse = active, Yellow shield = quarantined, Red = error.
queue.tip3: Hover over any pipeline step to see its name and description.

profiles.title: Profiles — Transcode Configuration
profiles.desc: Profiles define how media is transcoded. Use built-in presets for common workflows, or create custom profiles with specific codec, resolution, and quality settings.
profiles.presets: 6 system presets: Broadcast HD/SD, Web 4K/HD, Proxy, Social
profiles.custom: Create custom profiles with the editor sidebar
profiles.duplicate: Duplicate any profile as a starting point
profiles.settings: Set video codec, resolution, bitrate, audio LUFS, and VMAF threshold
profiles.tip1: System presets are read-only. Duplicate them to make changes.
profiles.tip2: Lower CPU preset = better quality but slower encoding.
profiles.tip3: The VMAF threshold determines the minimum acceptable quality score.

settings.title: Settings — Configuration
settings.desc: Configure output directory, concurrent jobs, GPU acceleration, notifications, theme, language, and more.
settings.general: General — output folder, concurrent jobs, GPU, notifications
settings.system: System — OS/hardware info, FFmpeg version, DB stats
settings.tip1: Set your Output Directory first — this is where all processed files go.
settings.tip2: More concurrent jobs = more CPU/RAM usage. Start with 2.
settings.tip3: Factory Reset wipes everything. Export your settings first if unsure.

logs.title: Logs — Diagnostics
logs.desc: View structured application logs with filtering by level, source, and time range. Export logs for troubleshooting.
logs.filter: Filter by level (DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR), source, or time
logs.export: Export all logs to a text file for sharing
logs.levels: Red border = ERROR, Yellow border = WARN
logs.autoScroll: Auto-scroll keeps the view at the newest entries
logs.tip1: Use Ctrl+Shift+E as a shortcut to export logs.
logs.tip2: Search for specific error messages to find patterns.
logs.tip3: Clearing logs does not affect the database or jobs.

assetDetailModal.noJobs: No processing found for this file.
